title: 10 best herbal teas for gum swelling

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Sage tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum swelling by neutralizing free radicals and promoting healthy gum tissue.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce gum swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.

Antimicrobial Properties: Sage tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing symptoms of gingivitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in sage have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm swollen gums, promoting healing and reducing pain. Regular consumption of sage tea may also help to strengthen the gums and prevent future swelling. This natural remedy can provide relief from gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums.
Salvianolic acid: This phenolic compound has strong anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce gum swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Carnosic acid: This terpene has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling by protecting against oxidative damage and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for gum swelling due to their antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections in the mouth.
Roots: The roots are used for teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make teas that can help soothe gum inflammation and promote healing due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the measured salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or cold, depending on your personal preference immediately.

Antioxidant activity: Purple coneflower tea's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that damage gum tissue, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can lead to swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in Echinacea purpurea also exhibit antioxidant properties, which help to combat oxidative stress and promote tissue repair. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside and isoechinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its ability to alleviate gum swelling and promote healing.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are used because they are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate gum inflammation.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm gum swelling.
Flowers: They are used because they contain alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a healthy plant in the morning.
Dry the harvested echinacea flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea flowers and leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes or until the desired color and flavor are achieved.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or cold within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part for making teas due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale root and place it in a tea infuser.
Boil 1 quart of water for 5 to 7 minutes to achieve a rolling boil.
Steep the zingiber officinale root in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to remove any remaining root particles.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy while it is still warm and fragrant.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried curcuma longa root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the curcuma longa root in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey to taste and stir well to dissolve the sweetness evenly.
Serve the tea immediately and consume within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial compounds: Peppermint tea contains antibacterial compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which help combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in peppermint tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling in the gums by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are pro-inflammatory hormones.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of mentha x piperita in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool water to remove dirt and impurities thoroughly.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried or 4 teaspoons of fresh mentha x piperita leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes or until the desired flavor is reached.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve immediately after steeping is complete.

remedy_recipe: Gather 28g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container for future use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried foeniculum vulgare roots and stems by selecting fresh organic material.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried pistacia lentiscus leaves and measure out two teaspoons for every cup of boiling water.

Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or mug.

Dry the harvested bark using sun drying or oven drying at low heat for 24 hours.
Crush 10-15 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark into small pieces to increase surface area.
Steep 5 grams of crushed ulmus rubra bark in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then strain.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

Rinse the thyme leaves thoroughly with cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Combine 1 cup of boiling water with 2 tablespoons of dried or fresh thymus vulgaris in a large tea infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the tea hot and consume it three times daily, storing any remaining tea in an airtight container refrigerated for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with gum swelling because it contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds such as eucalyptol and cineole. These natural compounds work to reduce inflammation in the gums, relieving pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and further alleviating symptoms. By soothing and calming the gums, Tasmanian blue gum teas provides effective relief for those experiencing gum swelling caused by periodontal disease or other oral health issues.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Barks: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, barks have been traditionally used to alleviate gum swelling and toothache.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 250ml of water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before consumption.
Drink one cup of the tea three times a day for a period of seven days to alleviate gum swelling.
Store any remaining tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper teas helps with gum swelling because it contains bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and carotenoids present in bell peppers have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the mouth, particularly in the gums. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Bell pepper tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like capsicum, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for periodontal issues.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in bell pepper tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting oral health and reducing gum swelling.
Corticosteroids: Bell pepper tea also contains corticosteroid-like compounds that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, bell pepper roots help reduce inflammation and soothe gum pain.
Fruits: Bell pepper fruits contain a high concentration of vitamin C, which aids in collagen production and promotes healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 dried capsicum annuum leaves from your local spice rack or store.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried leaves and add it to a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Pour in 8 ounces of boiling water over the dried leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into another cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature or slightly warm before using it as an oral rinse twice daily for 30 seconds each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with gum swelling because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the goldenseal root powder in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Allow the tea to cool for 5-7 minutes before drinking it slowly and carefully.
Consume the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes after meals, for a duration of 6-8 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and puffiness in the mouth. remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers.

Add the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers to the hot water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal teas are conditions such as swollen tonsils, sore throats, and mouth ulcers. Herbs like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing swelling and discomfort. Other herbs like sage, peppermint, and chamomile can also provide relief from gum swelling-related pain and inflammation when consumed as teas or infusions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum swelling are a natural remedy that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums. These teas contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that work to soothe the affected area. The best herbal teas for gum swelling include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Salvia officinalis, Achillea millefolium, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ulmus rubra, Calendula officinalis, Althaea officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, gum swelling can lead to painful dental issues, difficulty eating and speaking, and even social isolation due to embarrassment about one's appearance.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with gum swelling is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and echinacea. Peppermint tea's antibacterial properties help reduce inflammation, while chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the swollen gums. Echinacea's immune-boosting properties aid in fighting off underlying infections. Drinking a warm blend of these teas 2-3 times a day can help alleviate gum swelling, reduce pain, and promote overall oral health. This natural remedy can also help prevent future gum issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it a natural remedy for swollen gums. The anthocyanins present in Echinacea purpurea, a type of antioxidant, help to combat free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation. Regular consumption of the tea may also boost the immune system, helping to fight off infections that can cause gum swelling and promoting overall oral health. This makes it a popular choice among those seeking natural gum care.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the gums.
Phenolic Acids (e.g., Chlorogenic acid): As antioxidants, these compounds help combat oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to gum swelling.
Caffeic acid derivatives: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate gum swelling and promote overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isobutyl amide, which is believed to reduce inflammation and swelling.
Leaves: Contain compounds like chicoric acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate gum swelling.
Flowers: High in alkylamides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, including gum conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh or 1/4 cup of dried flowers and leaves from the echinacea purpurea plant.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a warm place for 1-2 weeks or use a dehydrator.
Chop 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers and leaves into small pieces and place them in a tea infuser.
Steep the chopped echinacea purpurea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then drink 1-2 cups per day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation. Additionally, ginger's ability to increase blood flow and reduce oxidative stress may also contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating gum swelling. This makes Zingiber officinale tea a potential natural remedy for gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Shogaols: Shogaols possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the gums, making them an effective remedy for gum inflammation.
Zingiberene: Zingiberene has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help combat gum inflammation by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: A key part used to make teas for gum swelling, as they contain active compounds such as gingerol and shogaol that help reduce inflammation and pain.

Leaves: Though less commonly used than the rhyzomes or roots, ginger leaves are used in some teas due to their lower concentration of active compounds, which still provide some relief from gum swelling.
remedy_recipe: Choose high-quality zingiber officinale root with no signs of mold or damage.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of zingiber officinale root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground zingiber officinale root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Serve the zingiber officinale tea hot or chilled and enjoy within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling in the gums. The tea's high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids help to combat bacterial growth and inflammation, promoting healthy gum tissue. remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and the plant is in its prime condition.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dry place for at least 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves per 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving it in a cup to enjoy its flavor and benefits.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a reputable source.
Steep the dried flowers and leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired to enhance the flavor and nutritional value.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the autumn season when the plant is mature.
Clean the roots thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the cleaned roots into thin pieces using a sharp knife and grind into powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 1 liter of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of calendula flowers by cutting off the flower tops with scissors when they are in full bloom.
Dry the flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag and allowing them to air dry for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make the tea.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any remaining plant material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its mucilaginous compounds. When consumed, these compounds form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues. Additionally, the tea's demulcent properties create a cooling sensation, which can help to calm and reduce swelling in the gums. As a result, Althaea officinalis tea has been used for centuries as a natural remedy to alleviate gum swelling and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Help with gum swelling by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues.

Barks: Barks may be used in teas for gum swelling, although less frequently than roots or leaves, as they still contain mucilages that aid in soothing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of dried althaea officinalis root and stems from a trusted herbal supplier.
Rinse the dried herb in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried althaea officinalis herb for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the herb in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a cup to remove the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per 8 ounces of boiling water for a standard infusion dosage.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes to release their medicinal properties and oils.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves before serving to enjoy the herbal eucalyptus globulus tea hot or iced.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with gum swelling because its unique blend of flavonoids and terpenoids work together to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the gums. Quercetin: As a potent flavonoid antioxidant, quercetin helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, promoting healing and overall oral well-being.

Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing gum swelling.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan until it reaches a boil in 5-7 minutes.
Add the ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves in 1 minute.
Drink the tea immediately while it's still warm to experience the full benefits in 1-2 minutes.
